kbSources.name: facts.md
kbSources.content:
Redis supports pub/sub messaging.

Redis stores data in memory.

Redis supports persistence via RDB and AOF.

kbSources.name: perf.md
kbSources.content:
BM25 processes 10,000 queries per second on a single core.

Dense retrieval processes 500 queries per second on GPU.

turns.content: Compare BM25 and dense retrieval throughput.

kbSources.name: db.md
kbSources.content:
PostgreSQL supports ACID transactions.

PostgreSQL uses MVCC for concurrency control.

turns.content: Explain PostgreSQL's concurrency model. Also explain MongoDB's sharding strategy.

kbSources.name: limits.md
kbSources.content:
The free tier allows 1000 API calls per day.

The pro tier allows 100,000 API calls per day.

turns.content: We are on the pro tier.
How many API calls can we make per day?

kbSources.name: compare.md
kbSources.content:
MySQL is open source and widely used.

MySQL uses row-level locking.

PostgreSQL supports advanced data types like JSONB and arrays.

PostgreSQL uses MVCC instead of locking.

kbSources.name: troubleshoot.md
kbSources.content:
High CPU usage is often caused by unoptimized queries.

Memory leaks in Node.js are usually caused by unclosed event listeners.

Slow disk I/O can be caused by insufficient IOPS.

turns.content: Our Node.js server's memory keeps growing. What could be wrong?

kbSources.name: setup.md
kbSources.content:
# Redis Setup

Step 1: Install Redis using apt-get install redis-server.

Step 2: Configure redis.conf with maxmemory 256mb.

Step 3: Enable AOF persistence with appendonly yes.

Step 4: Start the service with systemctl start redis.

turns.content: How do I set up Redis?

kbSources.name: options.md
kbSources.content:
Option A costs $100/month and has 99.9% uptime.

Option B costs $50/month and has 99.5% uptime.

Option C costs $200/month and has 99.99% uptime.

turns.content: Our budget is $150/month and we need at least 99.9% uptime.

kbSources.name: glossary.md
kbSources.content:
BM25 (Best Matching 25) is a probabilistic ranking function used in information retrieval.

TF-IDF stands for Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency.

PageRank is an algorithm used by Google to rank web pages.
